title: Count Set Flags in a bool Vector
prompt_md:
## Goal
Read N boolean values (0 or 1) into a `std::vector<bool>`, then print how many are `true`.

## Input
- Line 1: integer `N` (1 ≤ N ≤ 20).
- Line 2: N space-separated integers, each 0 or 1.

## Output
- Print exactly one line: `True count: X` where X is the number of 1 values.

## Requirements
1. Use `push_back` to build the vector.
2. Use a `for` loop with an integer index to count `true` entries — do NOT use `bool&` in the loop (it will not compile).
3. Hidden tests: all zeros (count = 0) and all ones (count = N).

title: Sieve of Eratosthenes Up to N — Complete the Function
prompt_md:
## Goal
Complete the function `sieve` that fills a `std::vector<bool>` of size `n+1` to mark prime numbers, then `main` reads N and prints all primes up to N.

## Input
- Line 1: integer `N` (2 ≤ N ≤ 50).

## Output
- Print all prime numbers from 2 to N, one per line, in ascending order.

## Requirements
1. The function signature is `std::vector<bool> sieve(int n)`.
2. Inside `sieve`: create a `std::vector<bool>` of size `n+1` initialised to `true`, set index 0 and 1 to `false`, then for each `i` from 2 to `n`, if `is_prime[i]` is still `true`, mark all multiples of `i` (starting at `i*i` or `2*i`) as `false`.
3. `main` calls `sieve(n)` and prints every index where the entry is `true`.
4. Hidden tests: N=2 (only one prime) and N=30.
